issues.body: The new consumeResultStream(streamPromise) awaits store.queryBindings(query) and then does `for await (const _row of stream) rows++`. store.queryBindings() resolves to the RDF/JS ResultStream produced by asResultStream()/ResultStream (js/src/result-stream.ts). That class is an EventEmitter-style pull stream — it exposes on()/read()/pause()/resume()/destroy() but implements NEITHER [Symbol.asyncIterator] NOR [Symbol.iterator] (grep confirms no Symbol.* iterator method; all existing tests in js/test/stream.test.mjs consume it via on('data')/read()/pause(), never via for-await). `for await...of` resolves Symbol.asyncIterator, then falls back to Symbol.iterator; finding neither it throws `TypeError: stream is not async iterable`. That throw propagates out of measureAsync()'s warm-up call and aborts the entire sparq bench block on the FIRST query's result-stream measurement — before the advisory JSON is ever printed. (The sibling path at line 102, `[...store.queryBindingsStream(query)]`, works only because queryBindingsStream returns a Generator, which has Symbol.iterator.) The added benchmark is therefore non-functional as written. Blast radius is limited to the manual, non-CI advisory bench sketch — the published @jeswr/sparq package and the fromBytes feature are unaffected — but the code in the diff does not run.
issues.fix_hint: Drain the RDF/JS ResultStream via its actual (EventEmitter) contract instead of for-await, e.g. wrap it in a Promise that resolves the row count on 'end' and rejects on 'error': `const rows = await new Promise((res, rej) => { let n = 0; stream.on('data', () => n++).on('end', () => res(n)).on('error', rej); });`. Alternatively give ResultStream a `[Symbol.asyncIterator]()` implementation (backed by read()/'readable') so for-await works, then keep consumeResultStream. Either way, run `node bench/vs-oxigraph.mjs` once to confirm the block completes and prints the advisory JSON.
issues.title: consumeResultStream() does `for await` on the non-async-iterable RDF/JS ResultStream — throws TypeError and crashes the bench

issues.body: fromBytes calls WasmStore.loadBytes / loadBytesWithBase, which only exist when the wasm is built with the bytes-ingest feature. The published build:wasm now enables it, but build:wasm:lean does not, so on a lean bundle fromBytes fails with 'WasmStore.loadBytes is not a function' rather than a clear 'byte ingest not available in this build' message. This is a documented, intentional tradeoff (the doc-comment notes dataset/compressed modes have no byte variants; the lean bundle omits byte ingest entirely) and does not affect the published package, so it is non-blocking. Carried over unaddressed from rounds 1 and 2.
issues.fix_hint: Optionally guard with `if (typeof WasmStore.loadBytes !== 'function') throw new Error('fromBytes requires a wasm bundle built with the bytes-ingest feature; use fromString on the lean bundle')` for a clearer failure on lean builds. Not required for correctness of the published package.
issues.title: fromBytes throws an opaque TypeError on the lean wasm bundle

summary: The PUBLISHED-PACKAGE change (SparqStore.fromBytes + WasmStore.loadBytes/loadBytesWithBase + build:wasm enabling bytes-ingest + d.ts/SKILL.md) is unchanged from round 2 and remains correct and complete: fromBytes delegates to the opt-in feature-gated wasm bindings (crates/sparq-wasm/src/bytes.rs) that fail-closed-decode UTF-8 of already-in-memory bytes then reuse the EXISTING Graph::load_str/load_str_with_base path — byte-for-byte equivalent to string ingest, zero new deps, non-vacuous native + wasm32 + JS integration tests. However, round 3 ADDS new benchmark code (js/bench/vs-oxigraph.mjs) that is broken: consumeResultStream() drains store.queryBindings(query) with `for await...of`, but that returns the RDF/JS ResultStream (js/src/result-stream.ts) which is an EventEmitter-style class exposing on/read/pause/resume/destroy and implements NEITHER Symbol.asyncIterator NOR Symbol.iterator (every existing test consumes it via the event API, never for-await). `for await` falls back Symbol.asyncIterator -> Symbol.iterator -> and finds neither, so it throws `TypeError: ... is not async iterable`, crashing the sparq bench block on the first query's result-stream measurement (before any advisory JSON is printed). Contrast the queryBindingsStream path (line 102) which works because a Generator has Symbol.iterator. This is a real correctness defect in the added code — it does not affect the published @jeswr/sparq package (the bench is a manual, non-CI advisory sketch), but the newly-added measurement is non-functional as written. The round-2 nit (fromBytes throws an opaque TypeError on the lean wasm bundle, which omits bytes-ingest) also remains. No injected instructions found in the diff.
